---
title: "Untitled"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-standard: ua-2
---

Markdown allows you to format text with *emphasis* and **strong emphasis**. 

You can also add superscripts^2^, subscripts~2~, and display code `verbatim`. 

Little known fact: you can also ~~strikethrough~~ text and present it in [small caps]{.smallcaps}. 

There is also [highlighted text]{.mark} and [Underlined Text]{.underline}.

For Creating accessible PDFs you still need Alt text for every image, specified with `fig-alt`.

```
![](elephant.png){fig-alt="A drawing of an elephant."}

# or multiple params
![](elephant.png){fig-alt="A drawing of an elephant." fig-align="left" width=20%}
```

To render the caption instead as alt text you can append a backslash to the line as detailed in the Pandoc documentation:

```
![A drawing of an elephant.](elephant.png)\
```
## Only work for HTML
For red text with yellow highlight: [red and yellow]{style="color: red; background-color: yellow;"}.

You can create custom classes or use Bootstrap classes (if rendering to HTML) to style text: 

- Text Colors (HTML): [Text in red]{.text-danger} or [Text in blue]{.text-primary}.
- Background Colors (HTML): [Highlighted]{.bg-warning}.
- Custom Class: [Custom text]{.my-custom-class} (Requires defining .my-custom-class in a custom CSS file). 





## Pandoc's Fenced Code Block
Sandwich the code between "fences" of at least three backticks! An optional language provides syntax highlighting.

```r
x <- c(1,100,10)
library(ggplot2)
plot(x, col="red")
```

Useful for displaying source code, not running it
Note that the language is not in { }


Block Elements: Lists (example lists)
The first list item with a @ marker will be numbered 1., the next 2., etc. The numbered examples need not occur in a single list; each new list using @ will take up where the last stopped. - the Pandoc Docs

(@) The first example.
(@) The second example.

Explanation of examples.

(@) The third example.

You can also label your examples: (@goodname).

![A figure about fenced div](~/Desktop/quarto_md_tips/Screenshot.png)\
